{"query":"India Italy Special Strategic Partnership Narendra Modi Giorgia Meloni joint roadmap cooperation sectors","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1767,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.newindianexpress.com/india/2026/May/21/india-italy-form-special-strategic-partnership","title":"India, Italy form special strategic partnership","content":"May 21, 2026 — The two countries also unveiled an ambitious target of raising bilateral trade from 14.25 billion euros in 2025 to 20 billion euros by 2029.
NEW DELHI: India and Italy elevated their bilateral relationship to a ‘special strategic partnership’, deepening cooperation across trade, defence, technology, connectivity and maritime security as Prime Minister Narendra Modi held extensive talks with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni in Rome."},{"url":"https://www.esteri.it/en/sala_stampa/archivionotizie/interviste/2026/05/i-rapporti-tra-india-e-italia-entrano-nella-fase-di-partnership-strategica-speciale-durante-la-visita-a-roma-del-primo-ministro-modi-caratterizzata-dallimec-e-dallaccordo-di-lib/","title":"India-Italy ties enter “Special Strategic Partnership” phase during PM Modi’s Rome visit, defined by IMEC, EU FTA – Ministero degli Affari Esteri e della Cooperazione Internazi","content":"We also provide further impetus to collaborations within the IMEC framework and in the African continent.
India and Italy have significantly upgraded bilateral ties in recent years.
During Prime Minister Modi’s visit, our countries elevated their relations to a Special Strategic Partnership, building on the Italy-India Joint Strategic Action Plan 2025–2029.
Cooperation is also growing strongly in defence, supported by the Defence Cooperation Agreement and industrial roadmap adopted by our Ministers of Defence.
Further growth potential also exists in agri-food, infrastructure, transport, innovation ecosystems, and high-tech sectors including space, defence and AI applied to robotics and manufacturing.
The EU-India Free Trade Agreement could mark a turning point in bilateral trade and unlock major opportunities for both economies.
Beyond its economic impact, the Agreement carries strong strategic value at a time of geopolitical uncertainty, strengthening ties between two major democracies.
IMEC can support the resilience of involved countries, adapting their trade, energy and digital connections to the uncertain international scenario.
Similarly to India, the Gulf is crucial for our energy security as we import a significant share of our gas and oil from the region.

Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani has just offered his condolences to Maldives’ Foreign Minister Iruthisham Adam...
